are you?

I mean, it,
you'll enjoy it any time but, um, the weather is certainly much nicer in the, the summer and fall.
Yeah, that's awesome.

I know how busy Mondays are in property management.
So I definitely appreciate it.
Uh And yeah, there's no pressure on today's call, just wanna have a back and forth conversation with you around market data, comp data, um walk you through a high level overview of our platform.
Um And then at the end to kind of get some feedback, see if potentially this could be a good fit.
And um if so we can discuss the next steps and if not, uh we can part ways as friends either way, not gonna hurt my feelings.

working with Jar.

that's correct?

got it.
Ok.
Yeah, Jar.

Uh I have like a handful of people that were kind of like below me to help kind of set up these uh qualified meetings and then my job is to have a back and forth conversation, see if it's worth going through an evaluation or not.
Ok.
Sure.
That sounds good.
Cool.
Um Well, no, it would, I got a couple questions for you off the bat just so I can get some uh some knowledge around your organization and processes in place.
So I'm curious um when it comes to market data and comp data, how do you all go about uh collecting that?
Like, you know, is it a manual process?
Do you have something in place.
Um And then what's the, what's kind of like the timeline on collection of that data?
Uh So for market data, well, I guess let's start with comp data.
Um Our teams, they, they do collect that on our behalf.
We feel feel that's a valuable process for them to understand where their comps are at for pricing.
So they do weekly um comp updates, rent updates and then for market data that that comes through from a variety of sources.
Um We have products with real page with Yardi, they typically send out various reports or have information available.
Um Also the the local multifamily associations they tend to put together reports.
So um there, there's a variety of different resources we utilize for for market information.
Um That, that we don't necessarily, it's not a manual process per se for us.
Um We just receive the, the reports from uh either the property management system or the, the local um
um multi family association.
Sure.
OK.
Um And how often are y'all receiving those reports?
I know you said the site teams are doing the comp data weekly.
But what about the overall market?
What, how often are those reports getting scheduled?
Oh, gosh, I think from Yardie Reel page, they've got monthly reporting available.
Um The the Multifamily Associations that's typically quarterly.
Ok.
And then let me ask you this, how are you guys leveraging that data?
Are you taking the accommodation of those reports and then the team's comp data.
And are you, are you pricing your units or floor plans based off of that or how do you all utilize the uh the market and comp data
uh for the comp data?
Um I mean, we don't price based off of it, but we do, we do reference it just to ensure that our, our rates are competitive.

Um So it, it, there is a, a pricing element but um it's not a, a direct 1 to 1 relationship there uh for the market data overall, just see what the trends are, occupancy, how we're trending in relation to the market.
Yeah, that makes complete sense.
Um And then when it comes to, when it comes to pricing and having those pricing calls, do you all utilize some sort of uh revenue management software or is the pricing done just in house manually?
What does that look like?
We do?
We use uh a revenue management software and, and you're right, we have um we have, we do biweekly pricing calls.
So um pricing analyst sets up the call connects with our property managers, portfolio, regional managers um every other week just to touch base on pricing and see if we need to make any changes.
Ok.
Yeah, I think that makes sense.
Yeah, I hear that a lot on uh these calls.
I'm on these calls all day, every day working with uh similar portfolios of, of y'all size and all the way up to kind of like the Willow Bridge Courtland A five.
I'm on calls with them as well.
Um
And the reason behind like me asking all these questions is uh especially this year, I feel like marketing comp data has been a very hot topic in our industry as a whole.
And a lot of these companies that I work with have all kind of issued their teams against manual call arounds or sharing of this data.

And um I just wanted to see if, if you know, your teams have been kind of uh dealing with that in terms of, has it been harder to for the call arounds to collect the comp data or, or the, or people not sharing as much or like the accuracy?
What uh kind of um
well, we don't, we don't call or reach out to different companies.
We, we typically will just reference their property website, uh the pricing they have listed online.
Um And that probably gets us about 95% of the, the information in some cases, if there's not a website or the pricing is not posted.
Um Typically a secondary resource like apartments.com or Craigslist will have that information.
So, um anyway, we, we've been doing that for quite some time, um seems to be fairly efficient for our teams to collect that information.
But um we don't reach out, I, I guess to leasing offices and, and ask for rents or occupancy information.
We're, we're really just looking for, for the rent trends, um, that are, that, that's available through websites.
Yeah, I think that makes total sense.
Um, and then I guess, like last thing for you and then I'll open it up to you and see if you have any questions for me.
I'll give you kind of the quick pitch on us and what we do.
But, uh, as far as looking to like automate and streamline and centralize all that data, um kind of where does this fall within your organization?
Is this something that has been talked about or this is a uh take the call because you just, you don't know what you don't know.
Yeah.

I thought I, you know, take a few minutes, learn what the product is and, and if there potentially could be a fit, I don't know that we have a specific need for market information.
But um again, things change on a regular basis in the industry.
So it's always good just to have an idea of what's out there and um you know, take a look at things and see if we're missing out on anything.

So, um, yeah, I, I wouldn't say this is a, an initiative or a priority on, on our end to find a new solution, but would certainly be interested in hearing about what apartment IQ is and, and what the product has to offer.
Sure.
Yeah, I'll, I'll, I'll walk you through it.
So kind of like a high level pitch on us.
Um Essentially what we give you is a live data feed on not only your assets but each specific comp set that you're tracking for each of your assets.

So when you log into our platform, um every single day and you're looking at the most up to date and accurate information and essentially it's a live data feed that lets you seize where each of your units is positioned within each specific comp set.
So um we can give you a high level data on properties, floor plans.
We get as granular as each individual unit.
We're tracking rent specials and concessions daily as well.
Um have about four years of historicals in the app that you can leverage for uh seasonality and trends.

Um We do have a section for kind of pricing recommendations that a lot of our clients like to use in conjunction with the revenue management tool where essentially we will flag if we believe a unit is overpriced or underpriced in this concept.
And we give you a lot of data to uh backfill that, why we're giving that recommendation.
So a lot of our clients have IQ up on their pricing calls in case their system is flagging something that they don't necessarily agree with.
Um But it's not a replacement by that of any means.
Uh It's more so for the day to day.
Really seeing where your units of floor plans are positioned within the market.
Sure.
Ok.
Ok.
